description: Distinguish primary issuance (IPO, seasoned offering, private placement) from secondary trading venues and evaluate call, continuous, quote-driven, and order-driven execution mechanisms

fields.name: market_type
fields.label: primary_ipo | primary_seo | primary_private | secondary_exchange | secondary_otc | secondary_ats

primary_transactions.ipo: First sale of shares to public; set by underwriters via book-building
primary_transactions.seo: Additional shares by listed issuer; dilutes existing holders
primary_transactions.private_placement: Sale to qualified investors; no public registration
primary_transactions.shelf_registration: Pre-register and draw down over time
primary_transactions.rights_offering: Existing shareholders receive rights to subscribe pro rata

underwriting.firm_commitment: Underwriter buys whole issue; bears unsold risk
underwriting.best_efforts: Underwriter acts as agent; issuer bears unsold risk

secondary_structures.call_market: Orders accumulated and crossed at single auction time
secondary_structures.quote_driven: Dealers post bid/ask; investors trade against dealer
secondary_structures.order_driven: Orders match based on price-time priority
secondary_structures.brokered_market: Dealer finds counterparty for illiquid instruments

well_functioning_markets.allocational_efficiency: Capital flows to most productive uses

classify_market_event.description: Record and classify a primary issuance or secondary venue

errors.message: market_type must be one of the supported primary/secondary categories

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume IPO allocations are random
anti_patterns.why: Underwriters favour long-term institutional clients; retail often receives over-subscribed deals that under-perform

anti_patterns.rule: Do not conflate OTC and unregulated
anti_patterns.why: OTC markets have rules, disclosure, and oversight — just different structure from exchanges

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore lock-up expiries after IPO
anti_patterns.why: Insider selling post-lock-up drives supply shocks; integrate into IPO post-trade models

goals.description: Distinguish primary issuance (IPO, seasoned offering, private placement) from secondary trading venues and evaluate call, continuous, quote-driven, and order-driven execution mechanisms

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
